flows.title: France P2P (SA) Current vs Future State

currentState.summary: Current-state P2P scaffold is now source-backed by the finalized CFTI P2P RCM workbook, CFTI project tracker structure (macro/micro views), and a parsed Trinidad France P2P document bundle (approval matrices, SOP/procedures, DocuShare guide, payment-process workbook, vendor-master workbook). Workbook-derived signals now confirm payment cadence, lead-time expectations, and Trustpair/Agicap/Sage payment handoff references. Operational step order and exception handling still require final walkthrough validation.

steps.label: Vendor setup / validation
painPoints: Exact current flow and evidence retention path not yet documented end-to-end.
Sage-based vendor master changes rely on manual/IT-dependent manual controls and email evidence (per P2P RCM gap examples).
controlNotes: CFTI P2P RCM includes explicit vendor-management controls (e.g., FR-PTP-02G, FR-PTP-03) and remediation-plan column.

Trinidad supplier-data workbook intake confirms vendor-master cleanup/reference material exists (supplier list and identification/bank/free-field tabs) and should be mapped to vendor setup/validation controls.
Trustpair validation is explicitly referenced in the P2P RCM and should be mapped to the operational process/evidence path.

steps.label: Invoice / coding / approval flow
painPoints: Workflow approvals not currently present in Sage.
controlNotes: Trinidad bundle includes approval matrices for POs and invoices plus signature policy documentation; these are now source artifacts for current-state approval mapping.
CFTI tracker micro view provides control-level status/gap fields (GAP, Overall Status, Expected/Effective Validation Date) that can anchor approval/audit-trail remediation tracking.

steps.label: Payment validation and execution
painPoints: Need exact handoffs and exception handling path documented end-to-end despite workbook process clues.
Need confirmation of current signer/validator roles and where control evidence is retained across Trustpair/Agicap/bank steps.
controlNotes: Trinidad payment-process workbook intake includes sheets referencing Trustpair process points, Agicap steps, and Sage payment-file/historical remittance handling; these should be converted into a source-backed payment swimlane sequence.

Derived workbook signals indicate two vendor payment campaigns per month (10th and 25th), a payment-file preparation lead-time note (~4 working days before value date), and Juliette/Fatma references in preparation roles.
Derived workbook signals also show an Agicap sheet and an EBICS signer-key mention, which are useful anchors for payment validation/signature-step mapping.

CFTI P2P RCM includes system classification and control type fields (Automated/Manual/IT-Dependent Manual; Preventative/Detective), which can be mapped to payment-stage controls once the process flow is confirmed.

futureState.summary: Target state should system-enforce approvals, reduce manual handoffs, and make control evidence traceable across vendor setup through payment.
targetChanges: Clear owner matrix by P2P step

evidenceNeeded: Trinidad process notes/docs/screenshots (bundle received and parsed; payment/vendor workbook signals extracted; PDF deep review pending)
Row-level extraction/classification from CFTI P2P RCM and project-plan micro view (headers confirmed; rows pending structured intake)

nextActions: Extract CFTI P2P RCM rows into structured issue/control list (Control ID, gap type, remediation plan, status, expected date).

Use Trinidad bundle intake derived signals to map initial payment-step current-state sequence (cadence, prep lead time, Trustpair/Agicap/Sage/EBICS references, Juliette/Fatma prep roles) before walkthrough
Deep-review approval matrices/SOPs/signature/currency PDFs to validate approval routing, signer thresholds, and evidence retention details
Draft side-by-side current-state France P2P step map
Tag pain points as ERP-addressable vs governance/process

flows.title: US LLC P2P Current vs Future State

currentState.summary: US P2P process is smaller-volume but highly manual and differs from France (e.g., bank payment flow, limited Agicap usage). Awaiting Kimberly/Matt materials to confirm exact steps and controls.

steps.label: Vendor setup / coding coordination
painPoints: Roles and evidence path need documentation.
controlNotes: Need current review/approval evidence path for vendor setup and coding changes.

steps.label: Payment initiation and execution
systems: Trustpair (used), no Agicap in LLC flow
painPoints: Manual upload templates, manual posting and reconciliations.
controlNotes: LLC receives less SOX attention today due size; need practical evidence expectations documented.

steps.label: Expense review / department allocation
painPoints: Manual review/allocation flow and timing dependence.
controlNotes: Manual review process may be a key future control-design target.

futureState.summary: Target state should improve US P2P timing, consistency, and evidence while preserving practicality for entity size and growth stage.
targetChanges: Consistent core P2P workflow/control model across entities where practical
Cleaner coding and department allocation flow
Improved evidence retention and review traceability

sourceArtifacts: US Finance Intro Notes (Matt/Kimberly/Adrian)
US P2P - ERP planning (sent email request)
nextActions: Map US flow and compare to France flow
Identify global-standard vs entity-specific process design choices
deckUseCases: Living Executive ERP Program Deck - P2P pillar visual
Board ERP Readout - operating complexity and de-risking

flows.title: Reporting / FP&A Data Flow Current vs Future State
flows.scope: French GAAP -> IFRS / SEC bridge, management reporting, FP&A dimensions

currentState.summary: Current state is quarterly-oriented and highly manual, with KPMG bridge dependency, Sage export limitations, and underused Adaptive/Interactive capability. Frederick emphasizes better quarterly quality before monthly scaling. The CFTI FSCP RCM now provides a source-backed control lens for reporting/close gaps and remediation plans.

steps.label: Source accounting / exports
painPoints: Missing data dimensions (e.g., country tag) impact reporting and cost center use cases.
controlNotes: Frederick identified missing reporting dimensions (including country tag) as a practical reporting/data-model blocker.
Manual data manipulation creates traceability and consistency risk.

steps.label: Bridge / consolidation / IFRS & SEC support
steps.owner: KPMG, Internal Finance / FP&A (split to document)
painPoints: Internal vs KPMG ownership not mapped by step.
controlNotes: FSCP RCM includes control-level fields for System, Automated/Manual, Preventative/Detective, and Control Gap Remediation Plan that can anchor handoff/control mapping.

steps.label: Management / departmental reporting and forecast visibility
controlNotes: FSCP RCM sample controls explicitly include Budgeting & Forecasting and Budget-to-Actuals Review (quarterly) with remediation-plan language and reviewer roles (Heads of Department/CFO/Board oversight).

futureState.summary: Target state should support a stronger quarterly reporting model immediately, with scalable data design for later monthly/commercial complexity.
targetChanges: Minimum reporting dimension model (entity/country/cost center/etc.) defined
Cleaner current-state reporting data flows and ownership map
Reduced manual bridge dependency where practical
Future-ready architecture for monthly/commercial scaling without forcing it immediately
erpAddressableChanges: Process/timing improvements from upstream P2P quality
nonErpChanges: Cadence decisions (quarterly vs monthly timing by stage)

evidenceNeeded: Row-level extraction/classification from CFTI FSCP RCM and project-plan micro view (headers confirmed; rows pending structured intake)
sourceArtifacts: CFTI attachment - Abivax FSCP FINAL RCM 22 December 2025.xlsx

flows.title: SOX / Controls / Auditability Current vs Future State
flows.scope: Finance + IT controls relevant to ERP program and auditability

currentState.summary: Manual process dependency and weak audit flow are recognized, and IT roadmap confirms SOX/compliance workstreams. CFTI source files are now received and confirm a macro/micro remediation tracker plus finalized P2P/FSCP RCM structures with control IDs, status, validation dates, and remediation-plan columns. ITGC RCM remains pending final validation.

steps.label: Identify control gaps / findings
painPoints: ITGC deficiency set is still pending final validation.
CFTI files are received but not yet fully extracted into a structured app issue list.
controlNotes: CFTI project tracker micro view includes Process, Sub-process, Control ID, GAP, validation fields, Overall Status, Expected/Effective Validation Dates, and comments/validators.

steps.label: Track remediation and evidence
steps.owner: Finance owner (remediation), IT support where technical
painPoints: Need row-level extraction to see all statuses/gap categories and owners in-app.
controlNotes: CFTI macro/micro tracker structure is now confirmed and should become the app's controls issue-register template.
ERP should address a subset; not all controls are ERP-addressable.

steps.label: Embed control-by-design into ERP implementation
painPoints: Need mapping from findings to design checkpoints.
controlNotes: P2P and FSCP RCMs both include Control Gap Remediation Plan fields and control metadata (system/manual type, reviewer/preparer roles) that can be mapped into ERP design checkpoints.

futureState.summary: Target state should map documented deficiencies to owner-dated remediation and clearly show which issues are solved by ERP design, process governance, or later phases.
targetChanges: Documented deficiency inventory and remediation ownership map
ERP-addressable control gaps identified and tracked to design decisions
Standardized evidence retention paths for critical flows
erpAddressableChanges: Integration monitoring and ownership clarity (where system-supported)
nonErpChanges: Audit program and testing cadence decisions

evidenceNeeded: Hema SOX findings/deficiencies (additional context and ownership confirmation)
CFTI remediation tracker row extraction and owner map
Examples of current evidence retention paths
